descriptions.value: Developing scalable pretraining strategies for foundation models, focusing on training data curation pipelines, tokenisation efficiency, and adaptive compute scheduling for large-scale transformer-based language models.

Leading cross-institutional collaborations on multilingual pretraining benchmarks and standardised evaluation protocols, with findings informing open-source tooling adopted across the research community.
Conducted theoretical and empirical research on large language model pretraining, including scaling law characterisation, optimisation dynamics, and data mixture strategies across diverse corpora.
Published three peer-reviewed papers at top-tier international venues including [Conference Name] and [Journal Name]; presented findings at international conferences and workshops.

researchExperience.department: Department of Earth Science and Engineering

descriptions.value: Research Assistant in Large Language Models, focusing on training and fine-tuning transformer-based models. Built and evaluated models using PyTorch and Hugging Face for tasks such as text generation and summarization.
Improved model performance through prompt engineering and efficient training methods. Supported research outputs with experiments, documentation, and reproducible code.
Developed and evaluated data selection algorithms for large-scale pretraining corpora, improving downstream task performance by optimising training data composition and domain coverage.
Collaborated with the language model team to prototype adaptive learning rate schedulers for billion-parameter models, achieving improved training stability across long-context settings.
Building and deploying scalable machine learning models using Python, PyTorch, and Azure ML. Developed data pipelines and supported production systems across NLP and recommendation tasks.

Optimised model performance and system efficiency through experimentation and monitoring. Collaborated with cross-functional teams to deliver reliable, production-ready ML solutions with reproducible workflows.

content.researchInterests: Foundation model pretraining, scaling laws, data-efficient pretraining, continual learning, and model generalisation. My research investigates how training data curation, architecture design, and optimisation strategies shape the emergent capabilities and efficiency of large-scale language models, with the goal of developing principled methods for building more capable and sustainable foundation models.
